74663.subject: Adyen and Stripe push deeper into Agentic Commerce after OpenAI’s checkout failure hands Google the protocol race …
74663.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/fintechpulse1090

Hey Everyone,
Good morning & happy Wednesday! Today we’re diving into payments giant Adyen, which is now pushing deeper into Agentic Commerce after OpenAI’s checkout failure (what Adyen Agentic is all about, how it compares with Stripe’s Agentic AI initiatives [ https://substack.com/redirect/cd73a595-a4df-4d94-a342-fb6311a7bfcd?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], & what’s one thing nobody talks about here + bonus dives into Google’s bid to become the ultimate OS for all e-com [ https://substack.com/redirect/9144ea55-481a-4d6d-9c3b-fe4b38937749?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], Visa & Mastercard’s AI challenges [ https://substack.com/redirect/30cbfdef-101d-46b3-9ff0-e50c7a6e2e65?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] & how to build your first AI Agent from scratch [ https://substack.com/redirect/139feb48-3247-4a6f-88dd-2ccc7ff78f85?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] inside), and Robinhood, which just laid of 10% of its employees without mentioning AI even once (what does that tell us, why it matters, & what it means for the future of fintech + bonus deep dive into Robinhood & Coinbase’s latest financials [ https://substack.com/redirect/2f44d8df-0626-4663-a10b-fe613dadd56c?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] and why now it’s the perfect time to productize yourself [ https://substack.com/redirect/429acfd5-8582-4242-97cb-75846f0b1678?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] inside). So let’s jump straight into the interesting stuff 🌶️
Adyen and Stripe push deeper into Agentic Commerce after OpenAI’s checkout failure hands Google the protocol race 🤖💳
The news 🗞️ Two of the world’s largest payment processors just escalated their agentic commerce bets within days of each other. Adyen [ https://substack.com/redirect/09edcb73-6692-45fa-97ca-30362fa58563?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] launched a full API suite for AI agent transactions. Stripe [ https://substack.com/redirect/b70fcbf3-fe0e-4e15-b00e-f97dda4531c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] expanded its own infrastructure through Amazon’s AWS.
Both moves come three months after OpenAI’s Instant Checkout collapsed (Walmart saw 3x worse conversion rates; fewer than 30 Shopify merchants ever went live) and weeks after Stripe joined Google’s Universal Commerce Protocol [ https://substack.com/redirect/136ace61-8ca6-4efc-b082-05c2f338ffa5?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], a quiet concession that the standard it co-built with OpenAI lost the race.
But the most telling detail is this - neither can point to real transaction volume from the channel they’re building for 👀
So let’s take a closer look at this, understand why it matters, and wh

74661.subject: 🚀 Elon's coding deal has a space problem
74661.body:
----------
######                      [Sign Up](http://utm.genai.works/r/gg2ubr5) | [Collaborate with us](https://6hhfxazcbpc.typeform.com/to/uXIl0IFH?utm_source=atlas&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=general_2026_05_11&utm_content=banner_post)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** SpaceX has been a public company for about a week and already dropped $60 billion on the coding app a million developers live in, and the reason it wants Cursor points straight up at space. Cursor, for its part, is done renting other people's AI and is building its own from scratch on Elon's machines. The money stories are wild too. OpenAI's secret books leaked and the loss is enormous, while DeepSeek pulled in $7.4 billion and handed its backers almost no say in return.

**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **SpaceX:** Why does a rocket company need the coding app on your laptop?

* **Cursor:** What's Cursor building now that Elon's paying the compute bill?

* **OpenAI:** How deep is the hole under OpenAI right before it goes public?

* **DeepSeek:** What did DeepSeek's backers give up to get in?


----------

----------
## [SpaceX is buying Cursor for $60 billion](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/06/16/spacex-launches-start-of-acquisition-spree-with-cursor-after-historic-ipo/)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/93ddf07f-91bc-486d-b34f-911a752d4a9a/Beehiiv_01_-_Jun_17.png?t=1781688930)
Caption: 

SpaceX has been a public company for four days, and it just agreed to spend $60 billion of fresh stock on Cursor, the AI coding app more than a million developers open every day. The deal closes by the end of September, and then a rocket company owns your code editor. Grok's behind on code, so "they wanted a better coding tool" is the easy read. That's also way too small to explain a $60 billion check.

Back in January, SpaceX asked the FCC for permission to put up to a million solar-powered satellites in orbit and run them as data centers, 500 to 2,000 kilometers up. The FCC took the filing and opened it for public comment. So yeah, SpaceX wants to move computing off the planet.

It gets weirder. In February, Musk told his xAI team he wants a factory on the Moon that builds those satellites out of Moon rock and flings them into orbit with a giant electromagnetic catapult. Two of Cursor's top engineers had already jumped to SpaceX in March to work on xAI and the lunar stuff.

Connect the dots. You can't send someone up to reboot a server floating in space, so running data centers up there means AI that can write, watch and fix the software on its own. That's the thing Cursor does. SpaceX is buying
